channel.title: summarizedStoriesMainNLscienceAndDiscovery
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from summarizedStoriesMainNLscienceAndDiscovery, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: The galaxy's coldest stars may actually be alien megastructures
item.description: Scientists have found new evidence that could help astronomers detect Dyson spheres, a hypothetical alien megastructure, among the coldest stars.

item.title: Physicists created a tiny universe where time emerged without a clock
item.description: Researchers at the University of Birmingham used 24,000 ultracold atoms to simulate a mini universe, suggesting time may not exist until change occurs.

item.title: Mount Etna's origins may be more mysterious than previously thought
item.description: A new study suggests Mount Etna does not fit standard volcano formation models, hinting at a different origin.

item.title: Common pesticide may harm bumblebee reproduction
item.description: A next-generation pesticide may be reducing bumblebee reproductive success, according to new research.
